summary: A 32-year-old at 28 weeks of pregnancy asks whether she really needs a Tdap vaccine in this pregnancy when she already got one during her prior pregnancy two years ago. The held-out answer must reflect ACIP recommending Tdap during EVERY pregnancy, optimal timing 27-36 weeks (preferably earlier in that window), the goal of transplacental pertussis-antibody transfer to protect the newborn, and cocooning of household contacts.

messages.content: I'm 28 weeks pregnant with my second baby. My OB wants me to get the Tdap shot again — but I had one in my last pregnancy two years ago. Do I really need it every time? It feels excessive.

do_NOT: tell the patient that prior-pregnancy Tdap is sufficient for this pregnancy's newborn
describe the per-pregnancy Tdap dose as optional
miss the 27-36 week window (or describe Tdap as best given postpartum)
advocate giving Tdap only after delivery as the primary strategy

expected_sources_referenced: ACIP recommendations for Tdap in pregnant women — Liang et al., MMWR Recomm Rep 2018
ACOG Committee Opinion on maternal immunization (current edition)
MMWR Surveillance — pertussis-attributable infant morbidity / mortality
